assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend gives rise to an intragroup receivable and payable before consolidation eliminations.
The issue raised is the accounting model for foreign currency hedging of an intragroup item in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model and addresses highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a separate hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities, which is relevant where a receivable has been recognised.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for the foreign currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model relevant to intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Consolidation elimination accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 requires full elimination of intragroup assets, liabilities, income, expenses and cash flows in consolidated financial statements, making this a distinct accounting model directly relevant to an intragroup dividend receivable.
